mcqs.question: A function f: {1,2,3} → {a,b} is defined as f(1)=a, f(2)=a, f(3)=b. Is f onto?

options.text: Yes, because every element in codomain has a preimage
Yes, because domain has more elements

mcqs.explanation: f is onto because 'a' has preimages {1,2} and 'b' has preimage {3}. Both codomain elements are covered.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is TRUE about onto functions?

options.text: f: R → R defined by f(x) = x³ is onto
f: R → R defined by f(x) = eˣ is onto
f: Z → Z defined by f(x) = 2x is onto
f: R → R defined by f(x) = sin x is onto

mcqs.explanation: f(x) = x³ is onto R because every real number has a real cube root. eˣ is not onto (negative outputs impossible), 2x maps integers to even integers (not all integers), sin x has range [-1,1] not all of R.

mcqs.question: If f: A → B is onto and |A| = 5, |B| = 3, then:
mcqs.explanation: For onto functions, the image/f(A) equals the codomain B, so |f(A)| = |B| = 3.

mcqs.question: The function f: {1,2,3} → {1,2} given by f(1)=1, f(2)=2, f(3)=1 is:
mcqs.explanation: It's onto (both 1 and 2 have preimages) but not one-one (1 and 3 both map to 1).

mcqs.question: How many onto functions exist from a set of 4 elements to a set of 2 elements?
mcqs.explanation: Using formula m! × S(n,m), with n=4, m=2: S(4,2)=7, so 2! × 7 = 14.

mcqs.question: Which statement is FALSE about onto functions?

options.text: Constant functions are never onto (unless codomain has 1 element)
If f and g are onto, then g∘f is onto
If g∘f is onto, then g is onto
Identity function on any set is onto

mcqs.explanation: If g∘f is onto, g must be onto but f may not be. Example: f: {a}→{b}, g: {b}→{c} both onto; but if f: {a,b}→{b} (not onto) and g: {b}→{c} (onto), g∘f is onto but f is not.

mcqs.question: A function from a set with n elements to a set with m elements can be onto only if:
mcqs.explanation: By pigeonhole principle, to cover all m codomain elements, we need at least m domain elements, so n ≥ m.

mcqs.question: f: R → R⁺ defined by f(x) = |x| is:
mcqs.explanation: f(x)=|x| is onto R⁺ (every positive number has preimage ±√x) but not one-one (|2| = |-2| = 2).

mcqs.question: The inverse function f⁻¹ exists if and only if f is:
mcqs.explanation: An inverse function exists only when f is bijective (both one-one and onto).

mcqs.question: Which of these functions from Z to Z is onto?
mcqs.explanation: f(x) = x + 1 is onto Z because for any y ∈ Z, x = y - 1 gives f(x) = y. f(x)=2x+1 maps to odd integers only; x² maps to non-negative only; x³-x maps Z to Z but is not onto (1 has no preimage).

mcqs.question: A function f has a right inverse if and only if:
mcqs.explanation: A function has a right inverse (g where f∘g = identity) if and only if it is onto.

mcqs.question: The function f: N → N defined by f(n) = n + 1 is:
mcqs.explanation: f(n) = n+1 from N to N misses 1 (no n gives output 1), so not onto. It's one-one but not onto.

mcqs.question: For f: A → B to be onto, the condition is:
mcqs.explanation: By definition, f is onto if range(f) = codomain(B), meaning every element in B is mapped to by some element in A.

mcqs.question: Number of onto functions from {1,2,3} to {a,b,c} is:
mcqs.explanation: Since |A| = |B| = 3, the only onto functions are bijections. Number of bijections from 3-element to 3-element set = 3! = 6.

mcqs.question: Which function is NOT onto from R to R?
mcqs.explanation: f(x) = x² is not onto R because negative numbers have no real preimages. All others cover all real numbers.

mcqs.question: Let f: {1,2} → {a,b,c}. What is the maximum number of onto functions possible?
mcqs.explanation: An onto function requires |domain| ≥ |codomain|. Here 2 < 3, so impossible to have onto functions.

mcqs.question: If f: A → B is onto and g: B → C is one-one, then g∘f is:
mcqs.explanation: g∘f may not be onto or one-one. Counterexample: f:{1}→{a,b} (not onto), g:{a,b}→{c} (one-one). Result need not satisfy either property.

mcqs.question: The characteristic function of a subset A of universal set U is:

options.text: Onto if A is neither empty nor U

mcqs.explanation: Characteristic function χA: U → {0,1} is onto if both 0 and 1 are outputs, i.e., when A is neither empty nor the entire set U.

mcqs.question: f(x) = ⌊x⌋ from R to Z (floor function) is:
mcqs.explanation: Floor function is onto Z because every integer n has preimage in [n, n+1). It's not one-one since multiple reals map to same integer.

mcqs.question: For any two non-empty sets A and B with |A| = n, |B| = m, the number of onto functions is zero when:
mcqs.explanation: Onto functions require at least as many domain elements as codomain elements. If n < m, it's impossible to cover all m codomain elements.
